rationale: Kim et al. (2011) is cited repeatedly beyond a background list: in the related-literature discussion, in the comparative Table 1, and in the map-validity argument about using search co-consideration to infer competition. The citing paper explicitly positions itself as methodologically close to Kim et al. in using online search data for market mapping, while identifying a specific limitation in prior work (no handling of multimarket membership products). It then presents M4 as a direct development of that stream.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Kim et al. (2011) as prior online-search market-mapping work and extends that line by introducing M4 to accommodate products that belong to multiple submarkets. It keeps the core idea that search behavior reveals competitive relationships, but replaces/augments the earlier mapping approach with a new clustering-plus-transformation framework and different embedding tools.
